zh.lifeSummary: 當William Beauchamp於1485年出生在英國可瓦爾時，他的父親Galfridus De Beauchamp40歲，母親Elizabeth de Trigoz29歲。他於1520年與Amy Haworth在英國英格蘭可瓦爾結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。他去世於1569年11月13日在英國享年84歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭艾可艾皮三。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Beauchamp于1485年出生在英国可瓦爾时，他的父亲Galfridus De Beauchamp40岁，母亲Elizabeth de Trigoz29岁。他于1520年与Amy Haworth在英国英格兰可瓦爾结婚。他们至少是2个女儿的父母。他去世于1569年11月13日在英国享年84岁，埋葬在英国英格兰艾可艾皮三。

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When William Beauchamp was born in 1485, in Cornwall, England, his father, Galfridus De Beauchamp, was 40 and his mother, Elizabeth de Trigoz, was 29. He married Amy Haworth in 1520, in Cornwall, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. He died on 13 November 1569, in England, at the age of 84, and was buried in Epping, Essex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Beauchamp est né en 1485, à Cornouailles, Angleterre, son père, Galfridus De Beauchamp, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth de Trigoz, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Amy Haworth en 1520, à Cornouailles, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Il est décédé le 13 novembre 1569, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 84 ans, et a été enterré à Epping, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Beauchamp 1485 in Cornwall, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Galfridus De Beauchamp 40 und seine Mutter Elizabeth de Trigoz 29. Er hat 1520 in Cornwall, England, United Kingdom Amy Haworth geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Töchter. Er starb am 13. November 1569 im Alter von 84 Jahren in England und wurde in Epping, Essex, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Beauchamp è nato il nel 1485 nel Cornwall, England, il suo padre, Galfridus De Beauchamp, aveva 40 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth de Trigoz, aveva 29 anni. Lui sposato Amy Haworth nel 1520, nel Cornwall, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie. Morì il 13 novembre 1569, nel England, all'età di 84 anni, e fu sepolto a Epping, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: William Beauchampが1485年にイギリスコルンワルで生まれた時，父Galfridus De Beauchampは40歳，母Elizabeth de Trigozは29歳であった。1520年にイギリスイギリスコルンワルでAmy Haworthと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の娘の親であった。彼は1569年11月13日にイギリスで84歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスエセックスエップイングにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Beauchamp(이)가 1485년 영국 코른월에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Galfridus De Beauchamp(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth de Trigoz(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1520년 영국 잉글랜드 코른월에서 Amy Haworth(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1569년 11월 13일 영국에서 84세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 핀그에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Beauchamp nasceu em 1485, em Cornwall, Inglaterra, seu pai, Galfridus De Beauchamp, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth de Trigoz, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Amy Haworth em 1520, em Cornwall, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 13 de novembro de 1569, em Inglaterra, com 84 anos, e foi sepultado em Epping, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Beauchamp родился в 1485 г. в местности корнвалл, Англия, его отцу по имени Galfridus De Beauchamp было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth de Trigoz, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Amy Haworth в 1520 г. в местности корнвалл, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Он умер 13 ноября 1569 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 84 лет и был похоронен в местности эппинг, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Beauchamp nació en 1485, en Cornwall, Inglaterra, su papá, Galfridus De Beauchamp, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Elizabeth de Trigoz, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Amy Haworth en 1520, en Cornwall, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijas. Murió el 13 de noviembre de 1569, en Inglaterra, a la edad de 84 años, y fue sepultado en Epping, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
